caption: A series of presentation slides for a "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" course, taught by "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay," is displayed. The slides focus on "Tutorial – 3," detailing a problem regarding a "Linde – Hampton system with Argon as working fluid." The problem specifies operation between "1.013 bar (1 atm) and 202.6 bar (200 atm) at 300 K," with a heat exchanger (HX) effectiveness of "100%." As the sequence progresses, specific goals are listed: "Ideal Work requirement" and "Liquid yield." The presentation then transitions to the "Ideal Work Requirement" section, introducing the formula "W/m = T1(s1 - s_f) - (h1 - h_f)" alongside a T-s diagram showing a constant pressure line. A table provides data for states 1, 2, and f, listing pressure (p), temperature (T), and enthalpy (h), concluding with a calculation beginning with "300(3.9 - 1.35) - (349 - 60)." The NPTEL logo appears in the bottom left corner.
